column1: Sentence
DISTRIBUTION MODULE
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION
PURPOSE
Refine the existing network distribution module:
* Add a universal interface for adding new exploits
* Determine the type of exploit to be used and design it to work through the interface
Add the BlueKeep exploit to the module.
MODULE DESCRIPTION
The module scans the local network and applies some kind of exploit on the found machines - the type is not known yet.
What is known is that the shell code downloads the load from http://galamatics.com/jomangi1.png and http://galamatics.com/jomangi2.png (most likely via DownloadFromUrlA) and runs it.
(most likely via DownloadFromUrlA) and runs it.
The organization of the shell code is unknown:
If it is exploit-specific, there may be a ROP.
If not, it may be searching for the necessary functions in the import table and working through them.
Scanning is performed on three types of network environment:
- Windows workgroup
- Windows domain
- LDAP
The module is designed according to the document module_HOWTO.
ADJUSTMENT
All refinements should be done within the module_HOWTO document.
The network scanning code does not require rework.
The following requires revision:
1) Exploit interface:
- The exploit must have a main method Exploit(const char* hostname) (this can be the address of the machine or its name)
- the network scanning code must be isolated from the exploitation code
- the module must be built as a constructor, from the right combination of network scanner and exploit variant, by conditional compilation.
The goal is to reuse the scanning code to create different modules based on it.
The combination of "each with each" is not needed (each exploit with each scan variant) - there is no goal to achieve full variability.
You should implement such a constructor in the following way:
- Using macros in the config.h file, we select which module we are building.
Using the same macros, we disable code that is not used in this build.
For example, if we use the FOO exploit, we do
config.h:
#define FOO
#undef BAR
foo.cpp:
#include "config.h"
#ifdef FOO
code here
#endif
- The name of the output file should be different for different variations
- It is best to use compilation profiles in Visual Studio for different variants
In this case, you can set preprocessor macros directly in the build settings for a specific project profile.
Create a different build profile for each variant.
2) The BlueKeep exploit should be added to the module according to the exploit interface.
Information on BlueKeep:
https://github.com/umarfarook882/CVE-2019-0708
https://github.com/n1xbyte/CVE-2019-0708
https://github.com/adalenv/CVE-2019-0708-Tool
https://github.com/gobysec/CVE-2019-0708
https://github.com/Ekultek/BlueKeep
https://github.com/robertdavidgraham/rdpscan
https://github.com/zerosum0x0/CVE-2019-0708
https://github.com/dothanthitiendiettiende/BlueKeep
https://github.com/Ekultek/BlueKeep/blob/master/bluekeep_poc.py
3) Module finalization
- obfuscate strings with Andrivet Advanced Obfuscator library, apply encryption from MetaString4.h
- obfuscate system calls via GetApi
- add the following events:
+ Module wormDll build %date% %time% started - when the module is started
+ Trying %sploit% on %hostname% (%addr%) - when an exploit is applied to the host
- make logging to a file in the current directory.
The name of the log is the same as the running module, but with the extension .log
Use the attached logging library.
- Logging should only occur in Debug, Release_logged build profiles.
Logging should be disabled in Release_nologs profile.
(by disabling macros LOG_TO_FILE, LOG_TO_CONSOLE)
